{{Quote|You are a wasted pawn of the Republic, young one. You could have been so much more, even with your wide-eyed innocence, your naïve love for others.|[[Kreia|Darth Traya]], to Mical|<ref name="TSL" />}}
'''Mical''', or "'''the Disciple'''", was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who had served as a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in the aftermath of the [[Jedi Civil War]]. A knowledgeable [[Jedi historian|historian]], he was one of several tasked with attempting to contact any remaining [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and convince them to return to the Republic. The Jedi Exile, [[Meetra Surik]] encountered him during her journey to gather the [[Lost Jedi]], and he joined the crew of the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', possibly due to the admiration he had for her since his youth. He became her informal [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], resuming the training he had begun in childhood, and becoming a [[Jedi Consular]]. After his travels with Surik, he devoted himself to rebuilding the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and would eventually sit on the re-established [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] as a Master. He also became one of the Order's greatest historians.

==Biography==
{{Quote|I mean… he's all right, I guess. He is like someone who watches too many holovids… or teaches them. He'd get beaten to death on Nar Shaddaa as soon as he landed, though.|[[Mira]]|<ref name="TSL" />}}
===Early life===
Mical was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] child, possibly from a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] colony. He was brought to the [[Jedi Enclave]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] to be trained in the ways of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. He became somewhat enamored of Meetra Surik after she taught his class of [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]] to hear [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] sing within others. Comparing her with Master [[Vrook Lamar]], Mical felt that while he was knowledgeable, he was not a natural leader or a mentor as was Surik.<ref name="TSL" />

By the time Mical came of age, the [[Mandalorian Wars]] were raging. Many [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] had gone to war against the wishes of the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]], including [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], [[Darth Malak|Malak]], and the Jedi that Mical had hoped would take him as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], Surik. Soon few masters remained and by the time that Mical was ready to begin apprenticeship there was no one left who was fit to train him. He was one of many who were denied the path of the Jedi at this time, in part due to the doubts he was having following the [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] of his mentor, so he decided to serve the Republic instead.<ref name="TSL" />

===Reunion with Meetra Surik===
{{Quote|Much has been forgotten in recent wars, and I fear that greater troubles shall stem from that loss of knowledge in the future. The destruction of the Jedi Academy on Ossus near the Cron Drift in the Sith War… the teachings of Master Arca, the adventures of Jolee Bindo on the Rimward Missions. All these things are in danger of being lost forever.|Mical|<ref name="TSL" />}}
[[File:Disciplepromo.jpg|thumb|right|Mical during his travels with Meetra Surik.]]
In the aftermath of the [[Jedi Civil War]], Mical was one of the few who maintained the distinction between the Jedi and the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. With the Jedi gone, he sought to preserve their history by studying their teachings and gathering artifacts. He had heard mention that one of the Jedi had gone to [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], so he went to the ruins of the Jedi Enclave, but found no trace of them. He found Meetra Surik instead, who told him of her search for the remaining Jedi Masters, and welcomed his company when Mical offered to apply his knowledge and skills to her quest, which seemed compatible with his.<ref name="TSL" />

Although history was Mical's specialty, he had some medical training as well, and served as a surrogate medic aboard the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]''. He also taught Surik a meditation technique to replenish her [[personal Force reservoir]].<ref name="TSL" />

At first Mical was not open about his past involvement with the Jedi, nor did he give out his true name, instead going by "Disciple." After Surik had gained his trust, he confessed to having met her as a young student at the Jedi Enclave, and told of how his connection to the Force had faded since then. Surik offered to train him in the ways of the Force, and he gladly accepted the offer, becoming her Padawan at last and taking on the class of [[Jedi Consular]].<ref name="TSL" />

As they traveled together, Mical was unable to hide his feelings for Surik. Though such things were forbidden to Jedi, Mical believed that the Jedi teachings were fundamentally flawed. He believed that the Jedi were too removed from everyday life, and from the very things that made them living, feeling beings. Still, though he never denied his forbidden love for Surik, it amounted to little more than flattery and innocent flirtation in the end. This placed him at odds with [[Atton Rand]], who also desired Surik's feelings.<ref name="TSL" />

[[File:Podisciple.jpg|left|thumb|The Disciple.]]
While aboard the ''Ebon Hawk'', Mical maintained clandestine communication with his Republic commanders, telling his true mission to no one—even Surik herself. When the party learned that [[Darth Nihilus]] was on his way to [[Telos IV]], Mical sent a message to his Republic superiors warning them of the imminent threat.<ref name="TSL" />

Throughout the journey, Mical pored over information about Revan and the Sith, discerning a pattern and strategy to Revan's attacks during the Jedi Civil War, and coming to the frightening conclusion that Revan's surgical strikes against the Republic were not meant to crush it into submission. Revan's tactics instead seemed more in line with conquering the galaxy to unite it under a single banner, possibly against an [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|unseen threat]]. [[Kreia]] walked in on him just as he had made this analysis and ambushed him, forcing him to forget his discoveries until she felt the time was right for him to remember.<ref name="TSL" />

After Surik met with the Council members, she separated herself from her companions. When she told Mical that the Council had informed her she was unknowingly forcing her companions to follow her, Mical comforted her. He told her that the Council made that judgment in arrogance because they denied what made them actual feeling beings. He also reassured her that he and all her other companions were following her because they chose to. He followed Surik to the end of their battle against the Sith. He was later left behind when Surik started her journey to find Revan.<ref name="TSL" />

===Famous Jedi Historian===
After her defeat at the hands of Surik, Kreia offered to share her vision into the future. She predicted that Mical would devote himself to rebuilding the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], and would eventually one day sit on the new [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]], though "reluctantly, as all good men do." Kreia's vision was proven right, as Master Mical was considered as one of the greatest Jedi Historians during the time of the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]]. His legacy was the archive he founded in the [[Cron Drift/Legends|Cron Drift]], which would be explored by adventurers and archaeologists, and [[Mical's holocron]] would be recovered and given to the Jedi Order.<ref name="TSL" />

[[File:JediConsular.jpg|thumb|right|[[Jedi Consular]] from [[Champions of the Force (Star Wars Miniatures)|''Champions of the Force'']].]]

==Behind the scenes==
Mical was created by [[Obsidian Entertainment]] as one of the companions for the [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. Like most companions, he was written by [[Chris Avellone]].<ref name="15 Years">{{WebCite|url=http://www.cinelinx.com/news/culture/knights-of-the-old-republic-ii-developer-chris-avellone-discusses-the-games-impact-15-years-later|text=Knights of the Old Republic II Developer Chris Avellone Discusses the Game's Impact 15 Years Later|work=[https://www.cinelinx.com Cinelinx]|archivedate=20191208220416|author=Maison, Jordan|date=2019-12-06|quote=I had to write almost all the companions, except for Mandalore and Bao-Dur (who was written by Michael Chu[)]}}</ref> He was voiced by [[Greg Ellis]].

The Disciple appears in cutscenes on Dantooine regardless of the player's gender, but he will be only available to join the player's party if the main character is female (male Exile is instead joined by [[Brianna]], the Handmaiden, much earlier in the game). Surik has a chance to train Mical, making him a Jedi Consular. (In the [[Champions of the Force (Star Wars Miniatures)|''Champions of the Force'']] miniature set, the [[Jedi Consular]] figure resembles Mical although no specific name is given.)

Throughout the game, he is only referred to as "Disciple"; the name Mical is mentioned only if the Exile is male—during Mical's conversation with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Carth Onasi]] (or [[Cede]] if Revan is set to be dark early in the game; however, the Mical-Cede conversation is bugged, as Cede's hologram does not show and Mical is talking to empty space). This cutscene will not be seen by the female Surik, since Mical has already joined her party, and therefore his name will not be heard by the player. In the MMO ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' players with the crew skill of Archaeology can send their companion to explore Mical's legacy.

===Alternate dark side ending===
[[File:Discipleds.jpg|left|thumb|Disciple's dark side portrait.]]
If Meetra Surik follows the path of the dark side (and is female), Mical will follow her and Darth Traya will predict that he will abandon all beliefs of the Jedi and instead devote himself to politics, becoming a wise and steady yet cold [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] of a Mid Rim colony (reminiscent of [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]). His dark image, as seen to the left, shows his blond hair growing gray, and his eyes are the yellow color of a Sith. Also, if Surik has enough influence with Mical, he can be trained to become a Dark Jedi Consular.

At some point in the original version of the dark side story Mical was supposed to be attacked by [[Atton Rand]], who had been corrupted by the dark side. He was also supposed to guide Surik to hidden Jedi and Sith [[Holocron/Legends|holocrons]]. Like a lot of other content, these ideas never made it into the final game, although they exist in the game files.

===Alternate male Surik ending===
After meeting Surik in the enclave, Mical returned to [[Khoonda]] and waited for the next transport off Dantooine. While still on the planet, he contacted Admiral Carth Onasi and reported that he had found Surik, whom the Admiral had been trying to track down since he escaped [[Citadel Station]]. It is possible that after Surik left known space, Mical was trained to be a Jedi when the Jedi Order began to rebuild.
